results.name: 05_conflicting_requirements
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: A simple rocket system model intended to describe a rocket with thrusters and fuel, and to relate the rocket design to a requirement about thruster count.
- Operational domain: Aerospace / rocket vehicle domain.
- Model scope: Small model fragment containing one requirement definition, one instantiated requirement, one system part definition, and one satisfy relationship.
- Known scope limitations: The model contains no behavioural model, no interface model, no protocol definitions, no allocations, no safety constraints, no numeric bounds beyond thruster multiplicity, and no requirement hierarchy beyond a single requirement. Checks depending on those absent views are assessed as not evidenced in this fragment and are not raised as false conflicts.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Numeric Constraint Conflicts - Examined the only explicit quantitative constraint (`thrusters : Thruster[3]`) and the single requirement text ("shall have 3 thrusters"); these are consistent and no contradictory numeric bounds are present.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Behavioural Conflicts - No state machines, activities, transitions, triggers, guards, sequencing constraints, deadlines, or liveness/safety behavioural statements are present in this model fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Interface & Protocol Conflicts - No ports, flows, connectors, signal types, rates, handshakes, retries, or protocol specifications are present, so no interface-side conflicts are evidenced.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Requirement-Level Conflicts - The model contains only one requirement instance (`thrustersReq : Thrusters`) with one stated condition, and no second requirement or parent/child decomposition exists to create a contradiction.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Allocation Conflicts - No functional allocations, processors, execution platforms, environmental zones, or connectivity allocations are modeled in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Safety vs. Functional Conflicts - No safety requirements, safety constraints, watchdogs, shutdown logic, or competing functional mandates are present.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Priority & Resolution - No actual conflicts were identified in the model, so no conflict-priority or resolution mechanism was required for this assessment.

Summary Table

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| None | 1.0 | HIGH | No conflicting requirements, constraints, or specifications were evidenced in this model fragment. |

Overall Assessment Score: 1.0
Model Status: Good - minor improvements only
